The '''AAC-1 speeder tank''' was a military [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] that was used by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Description==
The AAC-1 speeder tank was fast, with exceptional lateral maneuverability allowing it to slip sideways and dodge incoming fire. Equipped with reinforced hull plating, the AAC-1's only weak point was found to be the black coolers on the sides of the tank. Its primary weapons were forward-facing [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]] and [[Particle beam cannon|particle cannons]], which were capable of destroying an enemy tank with a small number of shots. A pair of rapid-fire [[Missile launcher/Legends|missile turrets]] were mounted on the top of the vehicle. The [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] used the laser cannons and beam cannons whilst the [[Gunner/Legends|gunner]] fired the missile turrets.<ref name="BF2-strat" /> The speeder tank was employed as an anti-aircraft combat vehicle,<ref name="CSWE" /> and employed a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] [[Engine/Legends|engine]] for movement.<ref name="DB" />

==History==
[[File:Sliced_Alliance_AAC-1.jpg|thumb|left|250px|An [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Slicer/Legends|slicer]] gaining access to an AAC-1.]]
Originally designed by [[SoroSuub Corporation/Legends|SoroSuub Corporation]], the vehicle that would become the AAC-1 was a prototype concept that never went into production. A SoroSuub designer sold the design blueprints to agents of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]<ref name="BF2-strat" /> and the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]]<ref name="CSWE" /> to pay off debts incurred from a night of [[Sabacc/Legends|Sabacc]]. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] found the vehicle to be effective when used in lightning strikes against military targets.<ref name="BF2-strat" />

The Royal Naboo Security Forces employed AAC-1s during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire's]] [[Battle of Naboo (Imperial Period)|assault]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]<ref name="BF2">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]''</ref> in [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],<ref name="Blog">{{Blog|holocron|22/comments|Movie Characters killed in the EU}}</ref> during which [[Queen/Legends|Queen]] [[Apailana/Legends|Apailana]] was [[Death/Legends|killed]]. Around [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], the Alliance and the Empire had AAC-1s stationed at their base on [[Polis Massa/Legends|Polis Massa]] when the Empire [[Raid on Polis Massa|raided]] the facility searching for the [[Death Star plans/Legends|Death Star plans]].<ref name="BF2" />

A variant, the [[AAC-2 hovertank]], was used by Rebel in battles such as the [[strike on Yavin 4]].<ref name="BF2" />

==Behind the scenes==
This vehicle replaces the [[Rebel combat speeder]] from ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]'', and shares the exact same backstory.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' {{1st}}
